applied_decisions.decision: Marked selected ambiguous features as Python/tigrbl focused for now with Rust not applicable.

classification_policy.manual_review_no_runtime_lane: Feature mentions both but lacks runtime_lanes evidence; review before split/copy/cleanup.
classification_policy.metadata_noise_rust_not_applicable: Feature mentions Rust only through runtime_lanes.rust.not_applicable or equivalent explanatory metadata; keep in tigrbl for now.
classification_policy.other_entities: ADRs, tests, claims, and evidence are listed for manual owner review because their proof semantics vary by path and purpose.
classification_policy.true_both_runtime_lane: Feature has runtime_lanes.python and runtime_lanes.rust both marked required/supported/planned.

purpose: First-pass classification of remaining SSOT rows that mention both tigrbl and tigrbl rust/rs after rust-only migration.
recommended_next_pass: For true_both_runtime_lane features, create or retain a tigrbl Python-owned row and create a tigrbl_rs Rust-owned row only when Rust proof is separately executable.
For metadata_noise_rust_not_applicable rows, keep in tigrbl and optionally remove verbose Rust not_applicable metadata in a cleanup pass.
For Rust-facing Python harness tests, decide whether the test is bridge coverage owned by tigrbl or Rust crate proof owned by tigrbl_rs before moving.
Keep parity ADR/claim/test rows only as coordination anchors unless they point at repo-local executable proof.
